commands.repair_reason: Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for security-scope fixes and real CLI proof; automation should not take over while the external proof gate is insufficient.; Needs attention: The patch is security hardening, but it leaves concrete secret-audit blind spots for managed config snapshots and JSON5-authored backups.; Review finding: [P2] Include managed config snapshots in backup audit — src/secrets/storage-scan... (sha=1dae6ecdb490e76603d6dd02bc59d077a5e7208f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds adjacent config backup discovery, scans those files in `openclaw secrets audit`, and adds a regression test for a stale plaintext provider key in a backup.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: create an adjacent `openclaw.json.pre-update` or JSON5-formatted `openclaw.json.bak` with a plaintext provider key and the proposed scan path will not report it.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor or maintainer follow-up for security-scope fixes and real CLI proof; automation should not take over while the external proof gate is insufficient.

target.title: Audit config backups for plaintext secrets
target.body:
## Summary

/claim #11829

This closes a concrete remaining gap from the API-key protection roadmap: stale adjacent `openclaw.json` backups can still contain plaintext credentials after the active config has moved to SecretRefs. `openclaw secrets audit` now scans adjacent config backup files and reports plaintext credential targets found there.

## Changes

- Add `listConfigBackupPaths()` to find adjacent backup names such as `openclaw.json.bak`, `openclaw.json.bak.*`, and `openclaw.json.*.bak`.
- Extend secrets audit to scan those backup files with the same configured secret target registry used for the active config.
- Report plaintext findings with the backup file path and JSON path so users can clean the stale copy.
- Add regression coverage for a SecretRef-clean active config with a stale backup that still contains a provider `apiKey`.

## Real behavior proof

I created a real temporary OpenClaw state directory under this checkout with an active config using an env SecretRef and an adjacent `openclaw.json.2026-02-28.bak` containing a redacted stale plaintext key. Then I ran the audit implementation against that setup, not a unit-test mock:

```text
pnpm exec tsx -e '<script creates tmp/real-behavior-proof/.openclaw and calls runSecretsAudit(...)>'
{
  "status": "findings",
  "summary": {
    "plaintextCount": 1,
    "unresolvedRefCount": 0,
    "shadowedRefCount": 0,
    "legacyResidueCount": 0
  },
  "filesScanned": [
    "tmp/real-behavior-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json",
    "tmp/real-behavior-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.2026-02-28.bak"
  ],
  "findings": [
    {
      "code": "PLAINTEXT_FOUND",
      "file": "tmp/real-behavior-proof/.openclaw/openclaw.json.2026-02-28.bak",
      "jsonPath": "models.providers.openai.apiKey",
      "message": "models.providers.openai.apiKey is stored as plaintext in a config backup."
    }
  ]
}
```

## Validation

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts -t "flags plaintext secrets left in adjacent config backups"

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       1 passed | 23 skipped (24)
```

Notes:
- `git diff --check` passes.
- A full `pnpm exec vitest run src/secrets/audit.test.ts` run stayed in the repo transform/startup path for several minutes in this local environment, so I stopped it and kept the validation focused on the new regression.

## Bounty

TaskBounty task: https://www.task-bounty.com/tasks/fix-security-roadmap-protecting-api-keys-from-agen-uej5sq
target.author: lukaivanic
